release tag 4.39 resolves to an unsigned upstream source commit
the upstream container release workflow disables provenance and has no attestation step

controls.evidence: ADR-0020 accepts the exact upstream commit, tree, archive checksum, and immutable Dockerfile frontend for the bounded local lab

sha256:cde502bffee14bdcd735cb253c86a3ea56d0634a9a75574ff0b4657ca2daf299 passed the vulnerability gate before Cosign v3.1.1 keyless signing bound it to the exact issuer, identity, workflow name, workflow ref, workflow SHA, source SHA, trigger, run ID, and run attempt
the local Cosign bundle, registry-retrieved bundle, exact Rekor v1 response, and verification payload are structurally linked and retained under bootstrap/seaweedfs/v4.39/evidence

Cosign and GitHub attestation verification bind the artifact to SeaweedFS 4.39 trusted arm64 build at .github/workflows/seaweedfs-arm64.yml, workflow/source SHA 20750d0e989118e1cdad8690290545e86f7219db, push run 29375069400 attempt 1

https://github.com/TommyKammy/Shirokuma/actions/runs/29375069400/attempts/1; every retained attestation record passed exact subject, workflow, ref, workflow/source SHA, trigger, runner environment, run ID, and run-attempt checks
the retained CycloneDX SBOM names the exact admitted digest and is hash-bound by release-evidence.json
Trivy v0.72.0 scanned the exact admitted digest with the retained database metadata and found Critical=0 and High=0

run 29375069400 attempt 1 sustained non-root weed mini for 10 seconds with the verified effective command, a read-only root, exact writable /tmp and /data tmpfs options, all capabilities dropped, no-new-privileges, and bounded PID/memory settings; the raw inspect and its bound summary are retained under bootstrap/seaweedfs/v4.39/evidence

run 29375069400 attempt 1 verified Crane v0.21.7 archive sha256 b6ee979d9411dfb05ce35ab9e156fe5de7def11a230764a7856ffa2eb971fa88 before registry login, linked the immutable candidate snapshot to the promotion record, and promoted sha256:cde502bffee14bdcd735cb253c86a3ea56d0634a9a75574ff0b4657ca2daf299 only after all gates and candidate evidence retention passed; the trusted tag is a non-authoritative publication pointer

assessment.rationale: The hardened Shirokuma source build uses a closed-world workflow and toolchain contract, binds source, Containerfile, workflow, trigger, runner substrate, run ID, and attempt to the exact digest, and verifies the effective runtime configuration rather than requested flags. Critical=0/High=0 scanning precedes signing and provenance publication; durable Cosign, registry, Rekor, SLSA, SBOM, scanner, runtime-inspect, candidate, promotion, and final evidence form one hash-linked lineage. Admission relies only on the immutable digest and committed final evidence; the trusted tag is non-authoritative and the upstream image remains blocked.

blockers.evidence: the parent must add a repository-source-build supply-chain record and prove the proposed SeaweedFS resident-ledger entry passes scripts/verify_supply_chain.py check-images before any runtime manifest is merged

requirements: add a source-build supply-chain record backed by the retained SBOM, Trivy, Cosign, and SLSA files
prove the proposed resident-image ledger entry passes make verify-security before adding Flux resources
pin the admitted exact digest in the resident-image ledger and Flux resources only after that gate passes
verify Flux reconciliation and functional S3 smoke on mac-studio-solo
record SSD impact and backup/export behavior
retain teardown and rebuild evidence for parent Issue #26
